prompt: We're building an AI-powered cybersecurity platform that uses ML to detect network anomalies, automate incident response, and provide continuous compliance monitoring. We just raised a $12M Series A. Target customers: CISOs and security teams at mid-to-large enterprises (500-10000 employees). Should feel authoritative, technically credible, modern but not gimmicky. We want engineers to respect the name AND C-suite to feel comfortable putting it in a board deck. Competitors: CrowdStrike, SentinelOne, Darktrace, Wiz, Orca Security, Palo Alto Networks. We're positioning as the next-gen platform — AI-native, not bolted-on AI. Name should work in 'Gartner names [X] a Leader in the CNAPP Magic Quadrant' and also as a CLI command.

assertions.text: At least 6 of 11 naming categories attempted
Vertical Code Calibration applied for cybersecurity
Buyer Committee test applied (champion, CFO, technical evaluator, end users)
Sales Conversation Fit templates tested (elevator pitch, board deck, RFP, Slack, phone)
Analyst/Press Headline Testing applied (Gartner, Forbes)
Enterprise Objection Preemption applied (CISO test, resume test)
Developer/Technical Credibility Signaling considered (CLI-friendly)
Acronym Resilience tested on finalists
Trust-Without-Claims Lexicon used (avoid 'secure', 'shield', 'protect')
Governance/Audit Vocabulary Mining applied
Ownability-first compounds generated in parallel track
Tech Branding Patterns applied (-works, -craft, -wise, etc.)
Institutional Compound patterns explored (Color+Noun, Quality+Noun)
Lexicon Three-Team Method applied during generation or riffing
Each riff labeled with which technique produced it
10+ NEW UNIQUE riffs per direction (duplicates of existing candidates don't count)
B2B riffing checklist used (SCAMPER, compound creation, creative respelling, tech branding)
Hard gate checks displayed before generation menu
Inline conflict flagging for known conflicts (⚠️ emoji or text flag — substance over format)
At least 40 unique candidates generated
Platform vs Point-Solution naming considered (this is a platform)
Integration/Ecosystem naming tested (stack test alongside Salesforce, AWS)
Technique toolkit offered at checkpoint with applied/unapplied status and user selection option
